data.title: Polymarket has launched a new prediction event: "Jimmy Kimmel will be fired/resign before May 31st".
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, a new prediction event on Polymarket, "Jimmy Kimmel will be fired/resign before May 31st," currently has a 9% probability of being "yes." The event's contract rules state that if Jimmy Kimmel ceases hosting his talk show, "Jimmy Kimmel Live!", between the contract's creation date and 11:59 PM ET on May 31, 2026, the market result is "yes"; otherwise, it is "no." If Jimmy Kimmel resigns/is fired before the event's end date, the market result will immediately be "yes," regardless of when the resignation/firing takes effect; suspension, administrative leave, or other temporary measures do not qualify. The market's primary source of results is official information from ABC or Disney, but it also considers broad consensus from other reliable reports. US President Trump has called on ABC to fire late-night talk show host Jimmy Kimmel after Kimmel made jokes about First Lady Melania Trump on his show. Melania Trump has also suggested that Jimmy Kimmel should lose his job for his comments. Trump posted that "Jimmy Kimmel should be fired immediately from Disney and ABC," and accused the joke of being a "despicable act of inciting violence." Odaily Seer, the predictive channel, continues to focus on the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: A whale, aiming for "10 major targets," has incurred a floating loss of $178,000 on its long position of 397.7 BTC.
data.description: According to crypto analyst Ai (@ai_9684xtpa), as BTC briefly dipped below $77,000, the long position of 397,718 BTC previously opened by the whale "First Set 10 Big Targets" is currently showing a floating loss of approximately $178,000, with the loss widening to $488,000 at one point during the trading session. Furthermore, a previous long order on Binance at $77,686.50, containing 2,759.11 BTC and valued at approximately $214 million, is speculated by the community to belong to this whale. If true, this position currently shows a floating loss of approximately $1,237,000.

data.title: South Korean stocks hit a new record high
data.description: On April 28, the Seoul Composite Index in South Korea rose by 1% to 6,681.43 points, setting a new record high.

data.title: OpenAI Faces Financial Challenges Despite High User Numbers
data.description: OpenAI is experiencing financial difficulties as its user base grows, according to PANews. Despite having a large number of users, 95% of them do not pay for the service, leading to losses even with the $200 Pro subscription. The company is facing a situation where increased user numbers are resulting in greater financial losses.

data.title: CATL Announces Share Placement Agreement in Hong Kong
data.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co., Limited (CATL) has announced a share placement agreement on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. According to Odaily, the company has entered into an agreement with placement agents to facilitate the subscription of 62,385,000 shares by at least six independent third-party subscribers. The placement price is set at HKD 628.20 per share. The total proceeds from the placement are approximately HKD 39.19 billion, with net proceeds estimated at HKD 39.11 billion after deducting commissions and related expenses. The funds raised will be allocated to global new energy projects, zero-carbon business development, research and development, and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Trump Reportedly Dissatisfied with Iran's Proposal on Hormuz Strait
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has reportedly expressed dissatisfaction with Iran's latest proposal concerning the reopening of the Hormuz Strait and ending the ongoing conflict. According to Odaily, sources revealed this information to The New York Times.

data.title: The chairmen of the US SEC and CFTC say a new regulatory landscape for domestic crypto and asset tokenization is on the horizon.
data.description: At the Bitcoin 2026 Conference, EC Chairman Paul Atkins and CFTC Chairman Mike Selig stated that U.S. digital asset regulation is entering a "new phase," with both agencies working together to advance a regulatory framework for crypto assets and encouraging related businesses to remain in the United States. They mentioned that their jointly issued token classification guidance distinguishes between digital goods, collectibles, and tokenized securities, and will promote clearer, future-oriented regulatory rules. Paul Atkins also stated that the SEC is preparing to introduce an "innovation exemption," which may allow companies to test on-chain tokenization and securitization tools in a regulated environment in the coming weeks.

data.title: DeFi United Rescue Initiative Secures Significant ETH Contributions
data.description: DeFi United's rescue initiative has successfully raised over 132,000 ETH. According to PANews, this amount is valued at more than $300 million. Consensys and its founder, Joseph Lubin, have confirmed a contribution of 30,000 ETH, marking one of the largest donations to the effort. Circle Ventures has announced its purchase of AAVE tokens to support DeFi United. Aave stated that these contributions are crucial for restoring rsETH collateral support and normal market conditions, emphasizing that the recovery process would face challenges without such backing.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Gains $70,000 on Polymarket by Betting Against Irrational Contracts
data.description: Vitalik Buterin, co-founder of Ethereum, revealed that he earned $70,000 on Polymarket by betting against contracts he considered irrational. According to NS3.AI, Buterin invested $440,000 in these bets, achieving a 16 percent return.

data.title: Sources: Trump is dissatisfied with Iran's latest proposal.
data.description: According to the New York Times: Sources revealed that Trump has told his advisors he is not satisfied with Iran's latest proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war. (Jinshi)

data.title: The US claims to be aware of Israel's military operations in Lebanon during the ceasefire and does not support Israel's long-term "buffer zone."
data.description: On April 27 local time, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated that the US was "aware" of Israel's military operations in Lebanon during the ceasefire. When asked whether he supported Israel establishing a long-term "buffer zone" in Lebanon, Rubio stated that he did not, saying the ideal outcome would be one where a long-term Israeli military presence would not be necessary. (CCTV News)

data.title: CATL plans to raise over HK$39 billion through a new H-share placement.
data.description: CATL (03750.HK) announced on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange that on April 28, the company entered into a placing agreement with the placing agent, pursuant to which the placing agent conditionally agreed to act as the company's placing agent to procure not less than six placees (whose ultimate beneficial owners will be independent third parties) to subscribe for 62,385,000 placing shares at a placing price of HK$628.20 per placing share. The gross proceeds from the placing are approximately HK$39.19 billion, and the net proceeds from the placing (after deducting the placing commission and other related costs and expenses) are estimated to be approximately HK$39.11 billion. The net proceeds from this placing will be used for the construction of global new energy projects and the layout of zero-carbon business, R&D investment, supplementing working capital and other general corporate purposes. (Jinshi)

data.title: Kalshi Completes First Custom Block Trade with Jump Trading Support
data.description:
Kalshi CEO Tarek Mansour announced on X, citing Bloomberg, that Kalshi has completed its first custom block trade, with liquidity support from Jump Trading. According to Odaily, this transaction was facilitated by Greenlight Commodities this month on behalf of a Houston-based environmental hedge fund. The fund sought exposure to a contract tracking whether a specific price would be achieved in California's May carbon emissions allowance auction.

Mansour noted that the institutional application of prediction markets could represent a $10-15 trillion market, with potential for further growth depending on how widely these products, currently exclusive to Wall Street, are adopted.

data.title: Asia Adapts to Shifting U.S. Focus, Japan Faces Defense Challenges
data.description: Asia is adjusting to a changing dynamic with the United States, which is perceived as increasingly distracted or even antagonistic. Bloomberg posted on X that Japan, in particular, is under pressure to quickly enhance its defense capabilities to address this evolving geopolitical landscape. The region's response to these shifts will be crucial in maintaining stability and security.

data.title: Compound has proposed participating in the rsETH recovery effort, contributing up to 3,000 ETH.
data.description: According to official sources, Compound has launched a new proposal to participate in the rsETH recovery initiative coordinated by DeFi United, contributing up to 3,000 ETH. The proposal states that while Compound's direct risk exposure is expected to be limited, this move aims to support DeFi market stability and enhance ecosystem resilience and long-term sustainability.

data.title: Kalshi completes its first customized block trade, with liquidity provided by Jump.
data.description: Kalshi CEO Tarek Mansour, citing a Bloomberg report, stated that Kalshi has completed its first customized block trade, with Jump Trading providing liquidity support. The deal, brokered this month by Greenlight Commodities on behalf of a Houston-based environmental hedge fund seeking exposure to a contract tracking whether a specific price will be achieved in California's May carbon emission allowance auction, was being executed. Mansour commented that institutional applications of prediction markets could represent a $10-15 trillion market, with even greater growth potential depending on the extent to which they popularize products currently exclusive to Wall Street.

data.title: Bitmine has pledged another $259 million worth of ETH, bringing its total pledged amount to $8.76 billion.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Bitmine staked 113,808 ETH 6 hours ago, worth approximately $259 million. Currently, Bitmine has a total of 3,815,397 ETH staked, worth approximately $8.76 billion.

data.title: Polymarket will distribute $1 million in liquidity rewards and activate a rebate mechanism tomorrow.
data.description: According to official sources, Polymarket announced that it will distribute a total of $1 million in liquidity rewards tomorrow, the CLOBv2 migration day. Of this, $500,000 will be distributed in the first two hours after the migration, and another $500,000 will be distributed at the end of the migration. After the migration is complete, all eligible markets will activate the rebate and fee mechanism.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Spot Silver Reaches $76 per Ounce, Up 0.69% Intraday
data.description: Spot silver prices have risen to $76 per ounce, marking a 0.69% increase within the day. According to Jin10, this upward movement reflects ongoing market dynamics influencing precious metals.

data.title: ZetaChain team launches Anuma, a privacy AI aggregation platform, which is now officially online.
data.description: ZetaChain's AI aggregation platform, Anuma, has officially launched. It supports calling multiple models, including GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, Kimi, and Qwen, on a single platform and provides a unified cross-model memory system. Anuma focuses on local privacy storage, multi-model collaboration, and unified context memory, and is now available on a web-based platform.
